title: IgE-Mediated Peanut Allergy: Mechanisms and Clinical Outcomes
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: This study investigates the immunological mechanisms underlying IgE-mediated peanut allergy in pediatric patients. We analyzed 342 patients over 5 years and identified key biomarkers predictive of severe anaphylactic reactions. Results suggest that early allergen exposure protocols may reduce sensitization rates by 40%.

title: Oral Immunotherapy for Milk Allergy: Phase III Clinical Trial Results
abstract: Phase III randomized controlled trial evaluating the efficacy and safety of graduated oral immunotherapy in 580 milk-allergic patients aged 4–17. Primary endpoint: sustained unresponsiveness at 52 weeks. 67% of treated patients achieved desensitization vs 4% placebo.

title: Allergic Rhinitis Prevalence in Urban Populations 2020–2024
abstract: Longitudinal dataset tracking allergic rhinitis incidence across 18 major cities worldwide from 2020 to 2024. Includes patient demographics, pollen counts, pollution indices, and symptom severity scores. Raw dataset for meta-analysis use.

title: Novel Biologic Therapies Targeting IL-4/IL-13 in Atopic Dermatitis
abstract: Keynote presentation slides from EAACI 2024 reviewing the clinical landscape of IL-4/IL-13 targeted biologics for moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis. Includes dupilumab, tralokinumab, and emerging pipeline agents with comparative efficacy data.

title: Gut Microbiome Dysbiosis and Food Allergy Susceptibility in Infants
abstract: Prospective cohort study of 1,200 infants from birth to 36 months examining the relationship between gut microbiome composition and development of food allergies. Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium abundance at 3 months predicted 73% lower food allergy risk at age 3.

title: Sublingual Immunotherapy Efficacy for House Dust Mite Allergy
abstract: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 42 randomized trials evaluating sublingual immunotherapy for house dust mite allergy. Significant reductions in rhinitis symptom scores (SMD -0.68) and rescue medication use observed across all age groups.

title: Airborne Pollen Monitoring Network: 2023 Annual Data Report
abstract: Complete dataset from 120 pollen monitoring stations across Europe for the 2023 season. Includes daily grain counts for birch, grass, olive, and ragweed. Correlated with hospital admission data for allergic rhinoconjunctivitis.

title: Anaphylaxis Management Guidelines Update 2024
abstract: Updated clinical practice guidelines for anaphylaxis recognition and emergency management. Key changes include revised epinephrine dosing protocols, updated criteria for biphasic reactions, and new recommendations for post-anaphylaxis observation periods in outpatient settings.

title: Allergic Asthma Phenotyping Using Machine Learning: A Multiomics Approach
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Integration of genomics, transcriptomics, and metabolomics data from 2,800 asthmatic patients to identify distinct allergic asthma endotypes. Five phenotypic clusters identified with differential treatment response profiles, enabling precision medicine approaches.

title: Contact Dermatitis Allergen Database 2024 Update
abstract: Updated structured database of 1,847 contact allergens with patch test concentrations, vehicles, prevalence rates, and cross-reactivity profiles. Includes newly identified sensitizers from cosmetics, industrial chemicals, and medical devices.

title: Immunological Basis of Latex Allergy in Healthcare Workers
abstract: Cross-sectional study examining IgE sensitization patterns to latex proteins in 1,500 healthcare workers. Hev b 5 and Hev b 13 identified as dominant allergen components. Powdered glove exposure correlated with 3.4-fold increased sensitization risk.

title: Wheat-Dependent Exercise-Induced Anaphylaxis: Case Series and Review
abstract: Case series of 78 patients with wheat-dependent exercise-induced anaphylaxis (WDEIA) presenting over 10 years. All confirmed omega-5-gliadin sensitization. Cofactors including NSAIDs and alcohol significantly lowered reaction thresholds.

title: Early Introduction of Allergenic Foods: LEAP Trial 5-Year Follow-up
abstract: Five-year follow-up of the Learning Early About Peanut (LEAP) trial. Children who consumed peanuts early maintained tolerance through school age. Sustained unresponsiveness rate of 74% in early-introduction group vs 19% in avoidance group at 72 months.

title: Allergen Immunotherapy Safety Monitoring Symposium Slides
authors: Dr. Anthony Fauci Institute Working Group
abstract: Symposium presentation reviewing post-market surveillance data on subcutaneous allergen immunotherapy safety across 48 allergy practices. Systemic reaction rate of 0.1% per injection. New risk stratification algorithm proposed for pre-injection assessment.

title: Eosinophilic Esophagitis: Molecular Subtypes and Dietary Therapy Response
abstract: Transcriptomic analysis of esophageal biopsies from 290 EoE patients identifies three molecular subtypes with distinct dietary trigger profiles. Subtype B responds preferentially to milk elimination; Subtype C requires six-food elimination for remission.

title: Venom Immunotherapy Outcomes: 10-Year Registry Data
abstract: Ten-year longitudinal registry data from 4,200 patients receiving venom immunotherapy for Hymenoptera allergy across 22 European centers. Complete dataset includes sting challenge outcomes, maintenance dose adherence, and long-term protection rates.

title: Biologics in Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria: Real-World Evidence Report
abstract: Real-world evidence report from 3,800 patients with chronic spontaneous urticaria treated with omalizumab. Complete response (UAS7=0) achieved in 52% at 6 months. Predictors of non-response identified including high baseline D-dimer and autoimmune comorbidities.

title: Cockroach Allergen Sensitization in Inner-City Children with Asthma
journal: Journal of Allergy and Clinical Immunology
abstract: Inner City Asthma Consortium study examining Bla g 2 (cockroach allergen) sensitization in 1,100 inner-city children with persistent asthma. High bedroom allergen levels associated with increased ER visits, oral steroid use, and school absences.

title: Drug Allergy Delabeling Program: Outcomes in 2,000 Penicillin-Labeled Patients
abstract: Prospective evaluation of penicillin allergy delabeling protocol across 14 hospitals. 95.2% of penicillin-labeled patients tolerated direct amoxicillin challenge without prior skin testing. Program reduced carbapenem use by 31% and C. difficile infections by 18%.

title: Climate Change and Allergen Seasonality Shift: 30-Year Analysis
abstract: Analysis of 30-year pollen monitoring data from 173 stations demonstrating significant advances in pollen season onset (average 20 days earlier) and increased total pollen loads (46% increase) attributable to rising CO2 and temperature. Ragweed season extended by 25 days in northern latitudes.

title: Pediatric Anaphylaxis in School Settings: Prevention Protocols Slide Deck
abstract: Comprehensive slide deck covering current evidence and recommendations for anaphylaxis preparedness in K-12 school settings. Includes emergency action plan templates, stock epinephrine legislation overview, and nurse training protocols.

title: Sensitization Patterns to Molecular Allergen Components in Southern Europe
abstract: Component-resolved diagnostic dataset from 6,800 patients across Spain, Italy, Greece, and Portugal. IgE reactivity profiles to 112 recombinant and natural allergen molecules. Raw dataset enabling geographic sensitization pattern analysis.

title: Dupilumab for Severe Pediatric Atopic Dermatitis: Long-Term Safety Data
journal: Journal of the American Academy of Dermatology
abstract: Three-year open-label extension study of dupilumab in children aged 6 months to 5 years with severe atopic dermatitis. Consistent EASI-75 achievement (89%) maintained throughout. No new safety signals; conjunctivitis rate 5.2%, injection site reactions 8.1%.

title: Allergic Bronchopulmonary Aspergillosis: Diagnostic Criteria Revision 2022
authors: International Society for Human and Animal Mycology
abstract: Expert consensus report revising diagnostic criteria for allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis. New ISHAM-ABPA working group criteria introduced with simplified scoring. Validated against 312 confirmed cases across 8 international centers.

title: Biomarkers of Allergic Inflammation: ILC2 and Type 2 Cytokines Review
abstract: Review slides examining ILC2 biology and type 2 cytokine networks as biomarkers and therapeutic targets in allergic disease. Covers IL-33, TSLP, IL-25 alarmins and their downstream ILC2 activation. Therapeutic implications for current and emerging biologic agents.

title: Nasal Polyposis and CRS with Eosinophilia: Surgical vs. Biologic Outcomes
abstract: Head-to-head comparison of endoscopic sinus surgery versus biologics (dupilumab, mepolizumab) in 400 patients with CRSwNP over 52 weeks. Biologics superior on SNOT-22 scores; surgery superior on objective endoscopy scores. Combination approach showed best overall outcomes.

title: Fish and Shellfish Allergy Cross-Reactivity Mapping
abstract: Comprehensive cross-reactivity dataset mapping IgE binding across 38 fish species and 22 shellfish species using ImmunoCAP and ISAC arrays. Parvalbumin sequence homology database and clinical reaction cross-reference for 890 seafood-allergic patients.

title: Allergen Avoidance in Pregnancy and Offspring Allergy Risk: Systematic Review
abstract: Systematic review of 56 studies examining whether maternal allergen avoidance during pregnancy reduces offspring allergy. Meta-analysis finds no protective effect of avoidance diets; some evidence suggests diverse maternal diet reduces offspring sensitization by 23%.
